items.title: Mechanisms underpinning the mental health impact of arts engagement
items.author: Pier Luigi Sacco
items.content: Engaging in the arts can have mental health benefits among clinical and non-clinical groups. In this Review, Fancourt et al. identify 50 causal processes by which arts engagement influences mental health outcomes and integrate them into a theoretical model, the ‘arts exposome’.

items.title: Human–AI interaction research needs to be embedded in psychological theory
items.author: Markus Langer
items.content: Human–artificial intelligence (AI) interaction research can become outdated quickly as technologies advance. To ensure cumulative insights that outlast the latest model release, research should meaningfully rely on psychological theory to explain, predict and anticipate human–AI interaction.

items.title: Paternalistic helping emerges early in humans
items.author: Rodney Tompkins
items.content: Paternalistic helping emerges early in humans

items.title: Spotlight on adolescent mental health
items.content: Research on adolescent mental health directly impacts the clinical, social and policy spheres. We are highlighting this important work by bringing together review, opinion and original research content on this topic in a dedicated Collection.

items.title: Determinants of individual navigation ability
items.author: Hugo J. Spiers
items.content: There is substantial variation across people in navigation ability, which is shaped by multiple factors. In this Review, Yavuz and Spiers organize these factors into lifetime, lifestyle and personal attributes, and discuss the cognitive and neural mechanisms of their influences on navigation.
